recurring_failures.issue: Raw ASR text too garbled for fuzzy matching with corrected text. When user provides corrected edit_decision.json, rebuild transcript from it directly instead of patching raw segments.
Python f-strings collide with JavaScript curly braces. Use json.dumps() for data + plain string concatenation for HTML/JS templates.

Zoom free-tier records stereo (2ch); in-person room mic is mono (1ch). Concatenating mixed channel counts without -ac 1 produces monster-voice artifacts (doubled duration, halved pitch). Always force -ac 1 on all segment extractions.

In-person room mic and Zoom computer audio have very different acoustic profiles (EQ, reverb, room tone). Even after loudness normalization, the listener hears a jarring shift between sources. Apply acoustic cohesion filtering (highpass, lowpass, compression) to all segments before concatenation, or consider voice-cloned TTS regeneration for full consistency.

Raw interview audio needs cleanup: trim long pauses (>0.8s) to natural breath gaps, and reduce (not eliminate) filler words like 'umm', 'uhh', '然后', '就是'. Keep fillers that serve as natural transitions. Use silenceremove filter for pauses; filler removal needs word-level ASR timestamps.
